steps.title: Identify the strongest clue
steps.content: The strongest clue is that the answer's conclusion must be directly supported by specific data in the table. Look for a choice that makes numerical comparisons between age groups using exact percentages shown.

steps.title: Apply the clue
steps.content:
D makes two specific claims comparing 50-64 and 30-49 age groups: higher personal relationship percentages (58 vs 53) and lower finance percentages (56 vs 63).

Both comparisons are directly verified by the table's data. The others cannot be verified: one assumes individual behavior the aggregate data doesn't show, one overgeneralizes to all US adults when only resolution makers are covered, and one contradicts the actual percentages.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
